name: VOD2MLIB
description: Generate .strm files for Dispatcharr VOD movies and series, with optional NFO metadata, batch processing, and scheduled auto-rescan that picks up newly-added episodes.
author: R3XCHRIS

fields.label: About
fields.description:
Workflow:
  1. Configure paths below.
  2. Actions → Scan → see catalogue totals.
  3. Actions → Generate Movies / Generate Series (start with Batch Size 10).
  4. (Optional) Turn ON Refresh Existing Series, set cron, click Apply Schedule for nightly auto-rescan.

Docs: https://github.com/R3XCHRIS/VOD2MLIB

fields.label: ── Paths & host ──
fields.description: Where to write .strm files and how media servers reach Dispatcharr.

fields.label: Root Folder for Movies
fields.help_text: Path inside the Dispatcharr container where movie folders will be created. Map a host folder to /VODS in your container.

fields.label: Root Folder for Series
fields.help_text: Path inside the Dispatcharr container where series folders will be created.

fields.label: Dispatcharr URL
fields.help_text: Full URL to your Dispatcharr instance (NOT localhost). Baked into .strm files, so it must be reachable from your media server.

fields.label: ── Movies ──
fields.description: Settings for the Generate Movies action.

fields.label: Batch Size (Movies)
fields.help_text: Number of movies to process in this run. Start small (10) to verify, then scale up.

fields.label: Generate Movie NFO Files
fields.help_text: Create .nfo metadata files alongside each movie .strm.

fields.label: ── Series ──
fields.description: Settings for the Generate Series action.

fields.label: Batch Size (Series)
fields.help_text: Number of series to process. Episodes are auto-fetched per series.

fields.label: Generate Series NFO Files
fields.help_text: Create tvshow.nfo and per-episode .nfo metadata files.

fields.label: Refresh Existing Series (rescan-friendly)
fields.help_text: Re-evaluate series that already have folders, picking up new episodes added upstream. Off = fast manual iteration (skip done series); On = scan everything for new content. Turn ON before clicking Apply Schedule for cron rescans of target 'generate_series'. Note: 'rescan_all' forces this ON regardless.

fields.label: ── Auto-rescan schedule ──
fields.description: Configure the cron job. Click Apply in the Actions tab to register or update.

fields.label: Auto-Rescan Schedule (cron)
fields.help_text: Standard 5-field cron: 'minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week'. Default '0 3 * * *' = every day at 03:00. Used by Apply Schedule.

fields.label: Scheduled Action
fields.help_text: Which action the scheduler should run on each tick. 'Full rescan' is recommended.

actions.label: [LIBRARY] Catalogue snapshot
actions.description: Count unique Movies and Series in the Dispatcharr database. Read-only.

actions.label: [GENERATE] Movies
actions.description: Process movies per Batch Size. Existing .strm files are skipped.

actions.label: [GENERATE] Series
actions.description: Create episode .strm files. See 'Refresh Existing Series' setting.

actions.label: [GENERATE] Full rescan
actions.description: Scan, then Movies, then Series — Refresh Existing forced ON. What cron fires.

actions.label: [SCHEDULE] Show status
actions.description: Show registered cron, last run, and total runs.

actions.label: [SCHEDULE] Apply / Update
actions.description: Register or update the cron task. Re-click after changing any setting.

confirm.title: Remove auto-rescan schedule?
confirm.message: This unregisters the periodic task. You can re-create it any time with Apply.

actions.label: [⚠ DANGER] Clean up Movies
actions.description: Delete plugin .strm/.nfo from Movies root. User files preserved.

confirm.title: Delete generated movie files?
confirm.message: This deletes every .strm and .nfo file this plugin created under your Movies root. User-added files (subtitles, posters, custom .nfo) in those folders are preserved. Continue?

actions.label: [⚠ DANGER] Clean up Series
actions.description: Delete plugin .strm/.nfo from Series root. User files preserved.

confirm.title: Delete generated series files?
confirm.message: This deletes every .strm and .nfo file this plugin created under your Series root. User-added files in those folders are preserved. Continue?
